headline: Leidos receives $617 million from U.S. Army for air defense launchers
summary: Leidos (NYSE: LDOS) received a $617 million award from the U.S. Army to build and deliver additional launchers for the Indirect Fire Protection Capability Increment 2 (IFPC Inc 2) system, the Army's newest mobile, ground-based air defense system. Combined with $356 million awarded in July and September 2025, Leidos now has nearly $1.2 billion in production contracts supporting the Army's future efforts to field this capability at scale.

content.title: Leidos receives $617 million from U.S. Army for air defense launchers
content.summary: Leidos (NYSE: LDOS) received a $617 million award from the U.S. Army to build and deliver additional launchers for the Indirect Fire Protection Capability Increment 2 (IFPC Inc 2) system, the Army's newest mobile, ground-based air defense system. Combined with $356 million awarded in July and September 2025, Leidos now has nearly $1.2 billion in production contracts supporting the Army's future efforts to field this capability at scale.

thumbnail.caption: Agile and modular, IFPC Inc 2 is designed and built to evolve with emerging technology to provide air-defense capability against rapidly evolving threats.
